title: Runbook Writing Foundation Lab Submission
description: Evidence template for writing operational runbooks in ARIA.

required_evidence.label: Runbook purpose
required_evidence.description: Explain what operational task, recovery process, or troubleshooting workflow the runbook covers.

required_evidence.label: Scope and assumptions
required_evidence.description: Define where the runbook applies, who should use it, and any assumptions or prerequisites.

required_evidence.label: Procedure steps
required_evidence.description: Provide clear numbered steps that another technician or student could follow.

required_evidence.label: Validation
required_evidence.description: Explain how the user proves the runbook worked or the task was completed.

required_evidence.label: Rollback or recovery
required_evidence.description: Explain what to do if the procedure fails or needs to be reversed.

required_evidence.label: Safety and secrets handling
required_evidence.description: State what information must not be pasted into the runbook, such as passwords, keys, tokens, cookies, or private data.

required_evidence.label: Professional summary
required_evidence.description: Write a short handoff summary explaining the runbook value and readiness.

safety_rules: Do not include passwords, API keys, SSH private keys, tokens, cookies, or secrets.
Do not document destructive steps without warning, validation, and rollback.
Write steps clearly enough for another person to follow.
Separate commands, expected results, and troubleshooting notes.
